title: 10 best herbal teas for inflamed gums
intro_desc: Herbal teas for inflamed gums are a natural remedy that uses the properties of herbs to soothe and calm inflammation in the mouth. These teas help by reducing swelling, killing bacteria, and promoting healing. Examples of herbal teas that have been shown to be effective include peppermint, chamomile, and sage. Drinking these teas regularly can help reduce discomfort, improve breath, and alleviate symptoms associated with inflamed gums, allowing individuals to live healthier and more confident lives.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower teas helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's main active compound, echinacea, works to reduce swelling and soothe irritated tissue in the mouth. Additionally, purple coneflower contains antioxidants that help combat free radicals, which can contribute to gum inflammation. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, this herbal tea provides natural relief from swollen, red, and painful gums, promoting a healthy and comfortable smile.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Purple Coneflower teas contain anti-inflammatory compounds like kaempferol and quercetin, which help reduce inflammation in the gums, alleviating symptoms of gum disease.

Antimicrobial properties: The tea's antimicrobial properties, attributed to the presence of triterpenoid saponins, help combat bacterial infections that can cause inflamed gums, such as gingivitis and periodontitis.

Antioxidant activity: Purple Coneflower teas' antioxidant properties, rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that contribute to gum inflammation, promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower contain a wealth of bioactive compounds, including triterpenoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe inflamed gums.
Barks: The barks of the purple coneflower contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps reduce inflammation and pain associated with inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried echinacea purpurea root and flower blend into a heat-resistant cup.
Boil one cup of water in a kettle or pot and let it cool for 5 minutes before pouring over the echinacea mixture.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the echinacea's active compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea into a separate cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any solids and sediment.
Enjoy the warm, slightly bitter tea immediately after straining, ideally three times a day for optimal benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Germander teas helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and discomfort. The tea's active compounds, such as geranial and linalool, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can contribute to gum inflammation. Additionally, germander teas' antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and promote healing. By drinking germander teas regularly, individuals can experience a reduction in inflamed gums and an improvement in overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Germander tea's antioxidants help reduce inflammation by neutralizing free radicals, which can damage gum tissue and contribute to inflammation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea contains compounds like rosmarinic acid, which have been shown to reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antimicrobial properties: Germander tea's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial overgrowth in the mouth, which can contribute to gum inflammation and disease by killing harmful bacteria and preventing their growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the germander plant contain bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate gum pain.
Barks: The barks of the germander plant possess anti-inflammatory properties that soothe swollen gums and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh teucrium chamaedrys leaves in the early morning or evening to ensure optimal potency.
Rinse 20-30 grams of dried teucrium chamaedrys leaves or 40-50 grams of fresh leaves in cold water to remove impurities.
Steep the rinsed herbal leaves in boiling water for 10-15 minutes to release bioactive compounds and flavonoids.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding solids and reserving the liquid.
Store the prepared tea in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stevia teas helps with inflamed gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal tea contains compounds that have been shown to reduce swelling, ease pain, and promote healing in the gums. Additionally, stevia's antibacterial qualities help combat gum disease-causing bacteria, further reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy oral environment. By drinking stevia tea regularly, individuals can experience relief from inflamed gums and maintain a balanced and healthy mouth.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Stevia tea's antimicrobial compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, help to reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which can contribute to gum inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants present in stevia tea, including polyphenols and glycosides, have anti-inflammatory effects that can help to reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums.

Astringent properties: Stevia's astringent compounds, such as tannins, help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding, which can be beneficial in soothing irritated gum tissue and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums.
Barks: Rich in antioxidants, barks have antimicrobial properties that combat bacteria causing gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stevia rebaudiana leaves from your garden or purchase dried ones from a reputable source.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried stevia leaves and add it to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the stevia leaves and let it steep for exactly 5 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup, discarding the solids immediately.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming it as a warm beverage within 15-20 minutes of preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with inflamed gums because it contains a unique combination of antioxidants, flavonoids, and saponins that work together to reduce inflammation and promote healing. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the gums, reducing redness and swelling. Additionally, the antimicrobial compounds in maidenhair tree teas have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can contribute to gum disease, allowing for a healthier oral environment.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Maidenhair tea contains high levels of antioxidants, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, promoting healthy gum tissue.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which work to reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums, allowing for faster healing.
Astringent properties: Maidenhair tea has astringent properties that help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding in the mouth, making it an effective treatment for bleeding gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with inflamed gums.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that work as natural antibacterial agents, combating the underlying causes of gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Rinse dried ginkgo biloba leaves with cold water to remove any impurities or debris.
Measure one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and add it to a teapot or infuser containing 8 oz of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the ginkgo biloba leaves to release their active compounds into the water.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or tea infuser to remove the leaves and any sediment.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, enjoying its subtle earthy flavor and potential benefits for oral health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage teas helps with inflamed gums because of its potent antibacterial properties, which reduce the amount of bad bacteria in the mouth that can contribute to gum inflammation. Additionally, sage's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The antioxidants present in sage tea also promote healthy gums by fighting off free radicals and preventing tissue damage. By drinking herbal sage tea regularly, individuals can enjoy a natural and effective way to soothe and heal inflamed gums, promoting overall oral health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Sage tea's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the mouth, which can contribute to gum inflammation and disease.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sage contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort in inflamed gums.
Salivary stimulants: Sage tea's volatile oils stimulate saliva production, which helps neutralize acid, remove bacteria, and flush out toxins that can contribute to gum inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sage plant are used to make medicinal teas for inflamed gums due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce swelling and eliminate bacteria that can cause gum disease.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the sage plant contain salvestrols, which have been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making them effective for reducing inflammation and preventing infection in inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried salvia officinalis leaves and pour into a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Heat one cup of boiling water and let it cool down for 5-7 minutes before pouring over the leaves.
Steep the leaves in hot water for 10-15 minutes to allow for maximum extraction of beneficial compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup to remove the solids.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature and drink within 30 minutes to an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon teas helps with inflamed gums because it contains a unique compound called cinnamaldehyde, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed as tea, this compound is absorbed into the bloodstream and travels to the affected gum tissue, where it reduces swelling and soothes discomfort. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections that can contribute to gum inflammation. As a result, regular consumption of this tea may promote healthy gums and alleviate symptoms associated with inflamed gums.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Ceylon cinnamon tea help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the mouth, which can contribute to gum inflammation and disease.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains cinnamaldehyde, a compound that has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, reducing swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums.

Antibacterial properties: Ceylon cinnamon tea's antibacterial properties help to combat bacteria such as Porphyromonas gingivalis, which are known to contribute to periodontal disease and gum inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Containing natural antibacterial and antifungal properties, rhyzomes prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections and promote healthy gum tissue.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh cinnamon bark of cinnamomum verum and organic peppermint leaves for the tea blend.
Boil one cup of water in a kettle or pot for five minutes to create a hot brewing temperature.
Add two teaspoons of ground cinnamon and one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves to the boiling water and steep for seven minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove loose herbs and solids.
Drink the warm tea immediately after preparation, or let it cool down completely before storing in an airtight container for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with inflamed gums because it contains compounds like glycyrrhizin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The tea's active ingredients also exhibit antibacterial properties, working to eliminate the underlying causes of gum inflammation. Additionally, licorice tea has been shown to stimulate saliva production, helping to neutralize acids and remineralize teeth, further promoting oral health and reducing inflammation in the gums.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: A compound found in licorice root that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which helps to reduce swelling and kill bacteria that can contribute to gum inflammation.
Flavonoids: A type of antioxidant found in licorice tea that helps to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can exacerbate gum inflammation and damage.

Triterpenoid saponins: Compounds that have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating effects, helping to reduce inflammation and modulate the body's response to infection, thereby promoting healing and reducing pain associated with inflamed gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and soothe gum pain.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Measure two teaspoons of dried licorice root into a large glass infuser or tea ball.
Heat one cup of filtered water in a medium-sized saucepan until it reaches boiling point at 212°f.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it steep for five to seven minutes allowing flavors to meld together.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a large ceramic mug or teapot.
Serve the tea hot, letting it cool down slightly before consumption within three to four hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. This natural remedy can help soothe and calm irritated gums, reducing redness and swelling associated with periodontal disease. Regular consumption of herbal ginger tea may also help prevent further gum damage and promote a healthier oral environment.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant compounds: Ginger tea contains antioxidant compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, including in the gums.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The anti-inflammatory compounds in ginger tea, such as gingerols and paradol, help to reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Warming and soothing properties: Ginger's warming and soothing properties can help to reduce gum inflammation and discomfort by increasing blood flow to the affected area and providing relief from pain and soreness.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, the underground stems of the ginger plant, contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums.

Roots: Ginger roots are used to make teas that soothe gum inflammation by reducing bleeding and discomfort, thanks to their ability to inhibit platelet aggregation and decrease prostaglandin synthesis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh ginger rhizomes weighing approximately one-quarter cup or 30 grams.
Wash the ginger thoroughly under cold running water then scrub gently with a vegetable brush to remove dirt and debris.
Slice the ginger into thin pieces measuring about one-eighth inch or three millimeters in thickness using a sharp knife.
Combine the sliced ginger with boiling water in a medium-sized pot steeping for seven to ten minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup discarding the solids and serving immediately while still warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric teas helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory compound, curcumin. This natural agent works by reducing inflammation in the mouth and body, thereby alleviating swollen, red, and painful gums. Curcumin's antioxidant properties also help to eliminate bacteria that can cause gum disease, promoting a healthy balance in the oral microbiome. Regular consumption of turmeric tea may lead to improved gum health, reduced bleeding, and fresher breath. Its natural antibacterial properties make it an effective natural remedy for combating gum inflammation and maintaining overall oral well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Turmeric's active compound curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums.
Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidants can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to gum inflammation and damage, promoting healthy gums and reducing the risk of periodontal disease.

Wound-healing agents: Turmeric's bioactive compounds have been shown to enhance wound healing by increasing collagen synthesis, improving tissue strength, and reducing scar formation, which can aid in repairing inflamed gum tissues.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Known for their potent anti-inflammatory properties, rhyzomes are effective in reducing gum inflammation and pain.
Roots: Rich in antioxidants and volatile oils, roots have natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe irritated gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one teaspoon of fresh curcuma longa roots or two teaspoons of dried roots.
Boil two cups of water in a large pot for five minutes.
Add the harvested curcuma longa roots to the boiling water and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids.
Let the tea steep for an additional five minutes before drinking it immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm teas helps with inflamed gums because of its unique ability to soothe and protect the mucous membranes. The mucilage, a natural gel-like substance found in slippery elm, forms a barrier that shields the gums from irritants and reduces inflammation. As the tea is consumed, it helps to reduce swelling, ease discomfort, and promote healthy gum tissue growth. Additionally, slippery elm's anti-inflammatory properties help to calm the body's response to infection, further alleviating symptoms of inflamed gums.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Slippery elm contains mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that can soothe and calm inflamed gum tissue, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in slippery elm tea have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, promoting healing and reducing the risk of further damage.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Slippery elm contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as beta-sitosterol and polyphenols, which can help reduce gum inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the slippery elm plant are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and calm irritated gums.
Barks: The inner bark of the slippery elm plant is high in fiber and antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ulmus rubra leaves and stems, washing them thoroughly with cold water.
Combine 2 tablespoons of dried or 4 tablespoons of fresh ulmus rubra leaves with 8 ounces boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbal goodness to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids and retaining the flavorful liquid.
Store the cooled tea in an airtight container, refrigerated for up to 3 days for optimal freshness.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat inflamed gums with herbal teas is to steep a strong infusion of peppermint, chamomile, or calendula in hot water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea and swish it around your mouth for 30 seconds before spitting it out. Repeat this process three times a day to reduce inflammation and promote healing. You can also add a teaspoon of salt to the tea for its antibacterial properties.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to inflamed gums that are treated with herbal teas are various oral health issues, such as mouth sores, bad breath, and dental plaque. Other conditions like ulcers, canker sores, and temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders also benefit from the anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties of herbal teas. Teas like chamomile, peppermint, and calendula are commonly used to soothe oral irritations and promote a healthy mouth.
